Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 1:

I think we want to get rid of flags like this instead.. why dt_attr_get() is not enough?

Patch Set 1:

> I think we want to get rid of flags like this instead.. why dt_attr_get() is not enough?

Strictly speaking, the patch does not introduce new flags, it only moves the existing common oo_destroyed flag from OSD to lu_object header.

The dt_attr_get() is not enough. According to the logs, the LFSCK engine hit failure before calling dt_attr_get().

Patch Set 1:

in the past we had number of issues with "flags" approach. this is why I think it's better to use methods (you can't make assertions, etc).

> The dt_attr_get() is not enough. According to the logs, the LFSCK engine hit failure before calling dt_attr_get().

why this is not enough?

Patch Set 1:

I'd think we should just add proper checks to osd_xattr_get()

Patch Set 1:

sorry, I don't understand.. existing osd_xattr_get() doesn't return ENOENT, right? so check like 	if (unlikely(!dt_object_exists(dt) || obj->oo_destroyed))
    RETURN(-ENOENT);
should be good?

Patch Set 1:

this is in the line with the other methods..

Patch Set 1:

The -ENOENT returned by osd_xattr_get_internal() will be converted as -ENODATA by osd_xattr_get(). The latter one is the API that will be called by upper layer, such as LFSCK.

Patch Set 1:

LFSCK then can distinguish ENOENT (object doesn't exist, see osd_attr_get()) and ENODATA - no EA. what's wrong?

Patch Set 1:

the important thing here is that an external flag (LO_whatever) being checked outside of a method is racy. and constructions like
if (object_exist(o)) osd_xattr_get() is racy unless you wrap it with a lock which I think we want to avoid in general.

Patch Set 1:

LFSCK can distinguish ENODATA and ENOENT, but the API is osd_xattr_get(), not osd_xattr_get_internal(). So the ENODATA is hidden by the osd_xattr_get() for some (my) unknown reason.

Patch Set 1:

typo, ENODATA is not hidden, but ENOENT is hidden.

Patch Set 1:

right, but this is correct behaviour as we (OSD users) expect ENODATA in case of missing EA, right?
so we have ENOENT available, so can you use for our purpose.

Patch Set 1:

For the case of missing EA, returning ENODATA is right, but for the case of object non-exist or destroyed, returning ENODATA is wrong. Current implementation mixed them together. My original patch tried to avoid changing the API. But since you suggest to adjust, then I am happy to make things more directly and easy.

Patch Set 1:

sorry, where do you see "mixture" ? osd-zfs returns ENODATA like osd-ldiskfs, AFAICS.
your patch changes the API as well..

Patch Set 1:

Currently, the caller of osd_xattr_get(), such as LFSCK, will get -ENODATA for the case of either the EA missed or the object non-exist. That is the "mixed" result. That is why my original patch tries to check that outside. But of course, if we can directly change the osd_xattr_get(), then this patch is necessary.

Patch Set 1:

hmm, I'm confused by "or the object non-exist" - how can it be if we never returned ENOENT before?

Patch Set 1:

According to current implementation, osd_xattr_get() (for osd-zfs) really does NOT return -ENOENT before for the case of object non-exists.
Uploaded patch set 2.

Patch Set 2:

parents.subject: LU-10902 mdd: force disable changelogs early and safely

commit.subject: LU-10922 osd-zfs: return -ENOENT if object destoryed
commit.message:
LU-10922 osd-zfs: return -ENOENT if object destoryed

Originally, if the target object does not exist or just destroyed,
the osd_xattr_get_internal() will return -ENOENT to the caller, it
is right. But one of the callers: osd_xattr_get(), converts it as
-ENODATA before returning to the upper layer by wrong.

This patch fixes it.

Signed-off-by: Fan Yong <fan.yong@intel.com>
Change-Id: I9bfd03c53dda165af37a4ae62b4efbc5875833e5
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/32042
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>

Reviewed-by: Lai Siyao <lai.siyao@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
